title: From Guessing to Placeholding: A Cost-Theoretic Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Code Completion
abstract: arXiv:2604.01849v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional proficiency in code completion, they typically adhere to a Hard Completion (HC) paradigm, compelling the generation of fully concrete code even amidst insufficient context. Our analysis of 3 million real-world interactions exposes the limitations of this strategy: 61% of the generated suggestions were either edited after acceptance or rejected despite exhibiting over 80% similarity to the user's subsequent code, suggesting that models frequently make erroneous predictions at specific token positions. Motivated by this observation, we propose Adaptive Placeholder Completion (APC), a collaborative framework that extends HC by strategically outputting explicit placeholders at high-entropy positions, allowing users to fill directly via IDE navigation. Theoretically, we formulate code completion as a cost-minimization problem under uncertainty. Premised on the observation th

translated_title: 从猜测到占位：一种面向不确定性感知代码补全的成本理论框架
translated_abstract: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码补全方面展现出卓越的能力，但它们通常遵循硬补全（HC）范式，即使在上下文不足的情况下也强制生成完全具体的代码。我们对300万次真实世界交互的分析揭示了这一策略的局限性：61%的生成建议在被接受后被编辑或遭到拒绝，尽管这些建议与用户后续代码的相似度超过80%，这表明模型在特定标记位置经常做出错误预测。基于这一观察，我们提出了自适应占位补全（APC），这是一种协作框架，通过在高熵位置策略性地输出显式占位符来扩展HC，使用户能够通过IDE导航直接填充。理论上，我们将代码补全建模为不确定性下的成本最小化问题。基于这一观察，我们...
tldr: 提出一种自适应占位补全（APC）框架，通过在高不确定性位置输出占位符替代硬补全，将代码补全建模为成本最小化问题，从而减少错误预测并提升用户效率。
en_tdlr: Proposes an Adaptive Placeholder Completion (APC) framework that outputs explicit placeholders at high-uncertainty positions instead of hard completion, formulating code completion as a cost-minimization problem to reduce erroneous predictions and improve user efficiency.
